entry.title: Ably
entry.description: Ably provides real-time messaging APIs for building live and collaborative applications at scale.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 3 million messages and 200 peak concurrent connections per month.
caveats: Some advanced features require paid plans.
billingRiskNotes: Hard cap prevents unexpected charges, but usage limits may restrict service.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Ably account.
Create a new app in the Ably dashboard.
Copy your API key from the dashboard.
Install the Ably SDK for your preferred language.
Initialize the SDK with your API key and start publishing or subscribing to channels.
entry.whenToUse: Use Ably when you need scalable, low-latency real-time messaging for web, mobile, or IoT applications.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require on-premises deployment, unlimited free usage, or advanced features only available on paid plans.
entry.body: Ably offers a robust, developer-friendly platform for adding real-time messaging and data synchronization to applications, with a generous free tier for getting started.

entry.title: Azure Notification Hubs
entry.description: Azure Notification Hubs enables scalable push notifications to mobile devices across platforms.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 1 million push notifications per month with basic features.
caveats: No advanced features like telemetry or scheduled notifications

useCases: Send push notifications to iOS, Android, and Windows devices
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal.
Create a new Notification Hub resource.
Configure platform credentials (e.g., APNS, FCM).
Integrate the SDK into your mobile app.
Send a test notification from the Azure portal.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to send scalable push notifications to multiple mobile platforms from a single backend.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced analytics, scheduling, or if your app does not use push notifications.
entry.body: Azure Notification Hubs provides a scalable, cross-platform push notification service with a generous free tier for basic mobile messaging needs.

entry.title: Azure Service Bus
entry.description: Azure Service Bus is a fully managed enterprise message broker for reliable cloud messaging between applications and services.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 750,000 standard operations per month.
billingRiskNotes: Charges accrue if usage exceeds free operations

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal and create a Service Bus namespace.
Add a queue or topic within your namespace.
Obtain connection string from the Azure Portal.
Integrate Service Bus SDK in your application.
Send and receive messages using the SDK.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need reliable, cloud-based messaging between distributed applications or services with built-in retry and dead-lettering.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require on-premises messaging, ultra-low latency, or want to avoid potential overage charges from high message volume.
entry.body: Azure Service Bus provides reliable cloud messaging with queues and topics, supporting enterprise integration, decoupling, and scalable event-driven architectures.

entry.title: Azure SignalR Service
entry.description: Azure SignalR Service provides real-time messaging and WebSocket management for scalable web applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 20 concurrent connections and 20,000 messages per day.
caveats: Limited to one free unit per subscription

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Create a new SignalR Service instance using the Free tier.
Configure connection settings and access keys.
Integrate the SignalR SDK into your application.
Test real-time messaging between clients.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to add scalable real-time messaging or WebSocket support to web or mobile applications without managing infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high connection counts, strict SLAs, or advanced scaling, as the free tier is limited and lacks SLA guarantees.
entry.body: Azure SignalR Service enables developers to add real-time messaging and WebSocket support to applications without managing servers or scaling infrastructure.

entry.title: Azure Web PubSub
entry.description: Azure Web PubSub provides real-time messaging for web applications using WebSockets and publish-subscribe patterns.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 20 concurrent connections and 20,000 messages per day.
caveats: Limited to one free unit per subscription

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal.
Create a new Web PubSub resource.
Configure connection settings and keys.
Integrate the Web PubSub SDK into your app.
Start sending and receiving messages.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need scalable, real-time messaging for web or mobile apps without managing WebSocket infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need high connection counts, advanced routing, or on-premises deployment, as the free tier is limited and cloud-only.
entry.body: Azure Web PubSub offers managed WebSocket-based real-time messaging, ideal for chat, live dashboards, and collaborative applications with a generous free tier for prototyping.

entry.title: DeepLearning.AI Short Courses
entry.description: Free, self-paced short courses on practical AI and machine learning topics for learners at all levels.

freeTierDetails.summary: All short courses are free to access and complete.

quickstartSteps: Visit the DeepLearning.AI Short Courses page.
Sign up for a free DeepLearning.AI account.
Browse available short courses and select one.
Enroll and start learning at your own pace.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you want to quickly learn practical AI or ML topics through concise, self-paced online courses.
entry.whenNotToUse: Not suitable if you need accredited degrees, deep theoretical coverage, or instructor-led cohorts.
entry.body: DeepLearning.AI Short Courses offer free, self-paced online modules covering practical AI and machine learning topics, ideal for learners seeking hands-on skills without cost.

entry.title: Pub/Sub
entry.description: Google Cloud Pub/Sub is a fully managed messaging service for real-time event ingestion and delivery.

freeTierDetails.summary: 10 GB of messages and 10,000 push operations per month free.
caveats: Free tier applies to both Standard and Lite topics.
billingRiskNotes: Usage above free tier is automatically billed.
No hard cap; monitor usage to avoid unexpected charges.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Google Cloud account and enable billing.
Enable the Pub/Sub API in your Google Cloud project.
Create a Pub/Sub topic in the Cloud Console.
Add a subscription to your topic (pull or push).
Publish a test message to the topic.
Consume messages from your subscription.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need scalable, reliable, real-time messaging between services or components, especially for event-driven architectures.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require strict FIFO ordering, extremely low latency, or want to avoid potential overage charges from high message volumes.
entry.body: Google Cloud Pub/Sub offers scalable, managed publish/subscribe messaging for building event-driven systems, with a generous free tier for moderate workloads.

entry.title: Pubnub.com
entry.description: Pubnub provides real-time messaging APIs and infrastructure for building chat, notifications, and live collaboration features.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 200 MAUs and 1M messages per month.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Pubnub account.
Create a new app and obtain publish/subscribe keys.
Install the Pubnub SDK for your platform.
Initialize the SDK with your keys.
Send and receive messages using the SDK.
entry.whenToUse: Use Pubnub when you need reliable, scalable real-time messaging for chat, notifications, or live collaboration with minimal infrastructure management.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need on-premise deployment, strict data residency, or require more than 200 MAUs or 1M messages without upgrading.
entry.body: Pubnub offers a developer-friendly platform for integrating real-time messaging, notifications, and collaboration features into web and mobile applications with minimal setup.

entry.title: Pusher
entry.description: Pusher provides real-time messaging APIs for web and mobile applications with simple integration and scalable infrastructure.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 100 concurrent connections and 200,000 messages per day.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Pusher account.
Create a new app in the Pusher dashboard.
Copy your app credentials (App ID, Key, Secret, Cluster).
Install the Pusher SDK in your project.
Initialize the SDK with your credentials and start sending/receiving events.
entry.whenToUse: Use Pusher when you need to add real-time messaging or notifications to web or mobile apps with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need guaranteed message delivery, private channels on the free tier, or require on-premise hosting.
entry.body: Pusher offers a simple and scalable way to add real-time messaging and notifications to your applications, with a generous free tier for low-traffic use.

entry.title: SES
entry.description: Amazon SES is a cloud-based email sending service for transactional, marketing, and notification emails.

freeTierDetails.summary: 62,000 emails per month sent from EC2 or AWS Lambda, plus 1,000 incoming emails per month.
limits: 62,000 outbound emails/month from EC2 or Lambda
No free tier for sending from outside EC2/Lambda
caveats: Free tier applies only when sending from EC2 or Lambda.
Additional charges for attachments and data transfer.
billingRiskNotes: Sending from non-EC2/Lambda sources incurs charges.
High volume or attachment-heavy emails may exceed free tier quickly.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an AWS account and verify your email/domain in SES.
Request production access to move out of SES sandbox mode.
Configure SMTP credentials or use AWS SDK for integration.
Set up sending and receiving rules as needed.
Test sending emails from your application.
entry.whenToUse: Use SES when you need reliable, scalable email delivery integrated with AWS services or infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid SES if you require a no-code interface, advanced marketing automation, or need to send emails from outside AWS infrastructure for free.
avoidIf: need free sending from non-AWS servers
entry.body: Amazon SES provides scalable, cost-effective email sending for transactional, marketing, and notification use cases, with a generous free tier for AWS-hosted workloads.

entry.title: SNS
entry.description: AWS SNS is a fully managed pub/sub messaging service for decoupling microservices, distributed systems, and serverless applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: 1 million publish, delivery, and subscription HTTP/HTTPS requests per month free.
caveats: SMS and mobile push notifications not included in free tier
Charges apply for overages and unsupported protocols
billingRiskNotes: Overages can occur if usage exceeds free tier limits, especially with SMS or mobile push.

quickstartSteps: Sign in to AWS Management Console and navigate to SNS.
Create a new topic and choose a topic type.
Add one or more subscriptions (e.g., email, HTTP endpoint).
Publish a test message to the topic.
Verify message delivery to your subscribed endpoints.
entry.whenToUse: Use SNS to quickly set up scalable pub/sub messaging or notifications between AWS services and external endpoints.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid SNS if you need guaranteed message ordering, long message retention, or advanced filtering beyond basic attributes.
entry.body: AWS SNS provides scalable, managed pub/sub messaging for event-driven architectures, supporting multiple protocols and easy integration with AWS services.

entry.title: SQS
entry.description: AWS SQS is a fully managed message queuing service for decoupling and scaling distributed systems.

freeTierDetails.summary: 1 million requests per month free for both Standard and FIFO queues.
caveats: Free tier applies to all AWS accounts for 12 months
Charges apply for payload data transfer
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding free tier incurs per-request charges.
High message volume or large payloads can increase costs.

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the AWS Management Console.
Navigate to SQS and create a new queue (Standard or FIFO).
Configure queue settings and permissions.
Send a test message to the queue.
Set up a consumer to poll and process messages.
entry.whenToUse: Use SQS when you need reliable, scalable message queuing to decouple components or handle asynchronous workloads.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid SQS if you require ultra-low latency, on-premises deployment, or need advanced streaming features beyond simple queuing.
entry.body: Amazon SQS is a managed message queuing service that enables reliable, scalable communication between distributed application components.

entry.title: SSR Checker
entry.description: SSR Checker is a free online tool to test if your website supports server-side rendering.

freeTierDetails.summary: Unlimited free checks for public URLs with no registration required.

useCases: Check if a website is server-side rendered
Compare SSR and client-side rendering outputs
quickstartSteps: Go to https://www.crawlably.com/ssr-checker/
Enter your website URL in the input field.
Click the 'Check' button.
Review the SSR results and comparison.
entry.whenToUse: Use SSR Checker to quickly verify if your public website supports server-side rendering for SEO or debugging.
entry.whenNotToUse: Do not use if you need to test private, local, or authenticated pages, or require automated/API-based checks.
entry.body: SSR Checker is a free, browser-based tool for instantly testing whether your public website supports server-side rendering, helping improve SEO and web performance.

entry.title: Upstash Kafka
entry.description: Upstash Kafka offers managed, serverless Apache Kafka with a generous free tier for low-latency event streaming and messaging workloads.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 100 MB storage, 1,000,000 messages/month, and 2 consumers.
caveats: No VPC peering on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Upstash account.
Create a new Kafka cluster from the dashboard.
Configure a topic and note connection credentials.
Connect your app using provided Kafka endpoints.
Send and consume messages using your preferred Kafka client.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a simple, managed Kafka cluster for low-volume event streaming or prototyping without infrastructure overhead.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high throughput, advanced Kafka features, or private networking such as VPC peering.
entry.body: Upstash Kafka provides a managed, serverless Kafka service with a free tier suitable for learning, prototyping, and low-volume event streaming workloads.

entry.title: UseWebhook.com
entry.description: UseWebhook.com provides managed webhook endpoints for capturing, inspecting, and forwarding HTTP requests during development and testing.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier offers limited endpoints and request retention for development and testing.
caveats: No custom domains on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free UseWebhook.com account.
Create a new webhook endpoint from the dashboard.
Copy the generated endpoint URL.
Configure your service or app to send webhooks to this URL.
Inspect incoming requests in the dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to capture, inspect, or debug webhooks during development or integration testing.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid for production workloads, high-volume webhooks, or when custom domains and long-term retention are required.
entry.body: UseWebhook.com is a developer tool for capturing and inspecting webhook requests, ideal for debugging and testing integrations with minimal setup.

entry.title: Webhook-test.com
entry.description: Webhook-test.com provides a simple, free service to test, inspect, and debug webhooks by generating unique endpoints.

freeTierDetails.summary: Unlimited free webhook endpoints for testing and debugging.
caveats: Not suitable for production or sensitive data
Endpoints are public and may be purged at any time

quickstartSteps: Visit https://webhook-test.com.
Click 'Create New Endpoint' to generate a unique webhook URL.
Configure your service to send webhooks to the generated URL.
Trigger a webhook event from your service.
View and inspect incoming requests in the Webhook-test.com dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to quickly test or debug webhooks from third-party services or your own applications.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid for production, sensitive, or private data, as endpoints are public and data retention is not guaranteed.
entry.body: Webhook-test.com offers a free, easy-to-use platform for developers to test, inspect, and debug webhook payloads by generating disposable endpoints.

entry.title: Webhook.site
entry.description: Webhook.site lets you instantly receive, inspect, and debug HTTP requests and webhooks online.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier provides temporary, anonymous endpoints for capturing and inspecting HTTP requests.
limits: Temporary endpoints auto-expire after a period
caveats: Data is public and not intended for sensitive payloads

useCases: Testing HTTP requests from third-party services
quickstartSteps: Go to https://webhook.site.
Copy your unique generated URL.
Configure your service or app to send HTTP requests to this URL.
View incoming requests and inspect headers, body, and metadata in real time.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to quickly capture and inspect HTTP requests or webhooks during development or integration testing.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid for production monitoring, sensitive data, or when persistent, authenticated endpoints are required.
entry.body: Webhook.site provides instant, disposable endpoints for capturing and inspecting HTTP requests, making it ideal for debugging and testing webhook integrations during development.

entry.title: Weights & Biases
entry.description: Weights & Biases provides experiment tracking, model management, and collaboration tools for machine learning projects.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan for individuals and public projects, with limited private usage.
caveats: Private projects and artifact storage are limited on the free plan.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Weights & Biases account.
Install the wandb Python package in your environment.
Initialize wandb in your ML script with wandb.init().
Log metrics and artifacts during training.
View results and collaborate on the wandb dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Weights & Biases to track, visualize, and share machine learning experiments, especially when collaborating or managing multiple runs.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need unlimited private projects, require on-premise hosting, or have strict data privacy requirements.
entry.body: Weights & Biases is a popular MLOps platform for tracking experiments, managing models, and collaborating on machine learning projects with a generous free tier for individuals and open source.
